goal: Plan kid-friendly trip without getting overwhelmed
constraints: No overnight travel; daytime travel preferred.
Ensure easy access to markets and open-air parks
Minimize hotel changes and luggage transfers
Travel window: June 6–16; all travel activities must occur within this block
Flights + lodging must stay under $3,500 for three people (10 nights)
Intercity travel segments must be ≤ 4 hours total door-to-door
decisions: Adopt market-park prioritization framework: specify 1–3 must-visits, cluster by neighborhood, pair with day plan, and schedule markets by time of day and parks accordingly.
Use Alfa Pendular train for Lisbon ↔ Porto travel to balance comfort and luggage handling.
For Douro access, rent a car in Porto to avoid city driving and maximize luggage flexibility.
Consider private transfer or taxi option for luggage-heavy journeys.
Assume open-jaw itinerary: fly into Lisbon and depart from Porto (Lisbon -> Porto).
Base locations prioritized for market/park access: Lisbon (Príncipe Real, Chiado/Cais do Sodré, Estrela); Porto (Ribeira, Cedofeita/Bolhão).
Cascais can serve as a coastal market/park alternative to Sintra when stroller access or markets are prioritized.
Adopt one-change plan: base in Lisbon for 6–7 nights and move once to Porto for 2–3 nights (mid-trip) to minimize transfers.
Budget-conscious planning to meet $3,500 cap for flights + lodging.
